title: Trump Calls Xi a ‘Friend.’ But He Left China Without Any Breakthroughs.
summary: The lack of concrete agreements with Beijing shows the risks of President Trump’s personality-driven foreign policy, which rests on the belief that he can defend U.S. interests through charm and force of will.

title: Trump Uses Taiwan Arms Sales as Bargaining Chip With China, in a Risky Move
summary: The president said a potential arms deal for Taiwan was a “very good negotiating chip” in talks with Beijing. His words raise questions about the reliability of U.S. support.

title: China Will Host Putin, Days After Trump’s Visit
summary: The Russian leader will go to Beijing on Tuesday for talks with President Xi Jinping. The Kremlin said he had watched President Trump’s visit closely.

title: How a Nature Cruise Turned Into a Nightmare
summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius set off alarms for a world still traumatized by Covid. For those on board, the danger was much closer.

title: In Congress, a Sexual Harassment ‘Minefield’ Persists, Aides and Former Staff Say
summary: Nearly 10 years after Congress instituted measures to make it easier for women to lodge harassment complaints, lawmakers and aides say the behavior is still rampant.

title: A Bitter Land Commissioner Race in New Mexico Over 9 Million Acres
summary: A Republican candidate for the state land commissioner post has Democrats worried that protected state and federal land could be opened to industry.

title: At Supreme Court, Virginia Democrats Pressed Legal Theory Embraced by Trump
summary: A long-shot emergency request to restore a voting map was the latest salvo in an all-out legal war over control of the House.

title: What to Watch in Saturday’s Republican Senate Primary in Louisiana
summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who has drawn President Trump’s ire, is fighting for political survival.

title: Which Countries Are Profiting From the Iran War Oil Shock
summary: An analysis of oil export data offers clues about which nations have benefited from higher prices, and which have lost a lot of revenue.

title: Fears Grow That Iran May Be Using Proxy Groups Beyond Mideast
summary: The charges against Mohammad al-Saadi in the United States have raised concerns that Iran could be working with its proxies to stage attacks outside the region.

title: Hamas’s Top Leader in Gaza Is Killed in Israeli Strike
summary: Izz al-Din al-Haddad took over the group’s military wing in Gaza last year. Hamas officials confirmed Mr. al-Haddad’s death in an Israeli attack.

title: How to Watch the Eurovision Song Contest Final
summary: The internationally popular event will be available to U.S. viewers, too, although not on domestic TV in some countries that are boycotting this year.

title: LIRR Labor Strike Shuts Down Busiest U.S. Passenger Rail Service
summary: This is the first strike on the service in more than 30 years. It comes after three years of failed contract negotiations, two federal interventions and a volley of last-minute bargaining.

title: Long Island Rail Road Workers Are on Strike. Here’s What to Know.
summary: America’s busiest passenger rail service shut down on Saturday after unions and transit officials failed to agree on a new contract.

title: What A.I. Kant Do
summary: Could we English majors have a future after all?

title: China’s Peak Is Now
summary: Does Xi Jinping see the world this way?

title: Finishing School
summary: It’s graduation season, a time of aspiration and anxiety. Today is about the aspiration — the wisdom to be gleaned from great commencement addresses.

title: Jack Douglas, Producer for Aerosmith and Lennon, Dies at 80
summary: A onetime Beatlemaniac, he helped shape the sound of hits like “Walk This Way” and “(Just Like) Starting Over.”

title: Man Stole Medieval Skull From Church and Sealed it in Concrete, Police Say
summary: The man disagreed with the 800-year-old relic’s display in a church in the Czech Republic and planned to throw it into a river, police said.

title: Drake Drops 3 Albums: ‘Iceman,’ ‘Habibti’ and ‘Maid of Honour’
summary: The chart-topping Canadian rapper released an anticipated new album, “Iceman,” on Friday and two previously unannounced LPs, “Habibti” and “Maid of Honour.”
